title: trisetum spicatum subsp. cumingii
intro: Trisetum spicatum subsp. cumingii, a subspecies of the Trisetum spicatum grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum subsp. cumingii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
